summary: Task 514 merge already landed — round-3 stash_failed feedback is stale (orchestrator merge queue may be replaying stale state)
detail:
Round-3 investigation (2026-04-07, step-7) conclusively confirms the task/514 merge is still in main and the current git state of main supports advance_main successfully. Nine verification checks all passed:

(1) `git merge-base --is-ancestor d7daa9841d main && echo ANCESTOR` → ANCESTOR.

(2) `git reflog main | grep -i 514` shows: cc2fded01b main@{29} (set_task_status(514=done)) and d7daa9841d main@{30} (merge task/514). Both in reflog.

(3) `git cat-file -t d7daa9841d` → commit. Object exists.

(4) `find -name '*MagicMock*'` → empty. No artifact directories.

(5) `ls orchestrator/` → only legitimate files.

(6) `git stash push -u -m 'task-514-round3-step7-verify'` → RC=0, stash created and dropped cleanly.

(7) `git stash push -m 'merge-queue: pre-advance test'` (EXACT advance_main command shape) → RC=0, 'No local changes to save'. Authoritative proof advance_main cannot fail at stash step.

(8) `git status --porcelain | wc -l` → 41 lines all `??`. No tracked modifications.

(9) `ls .git/index.lock` → No such file. No stale lock.

This is the THIRD consecutive stash_failed report despite the merge having already landed at d7daa9841d. Prior escalation: esc-514-86.
suggested_action: Check orchestrator merge queue persistence for task 514 — it appears to be replaying the original round-1 stash_failed without re-verifying git state. The merge commit d7daa9841d is already in main. Run `git merge-base --is-ancestor d7daa9841d main` to confirm, then mark task 514's merge queue entry as already-merged to stop the replay loop.
resolution:
Found and broke the replay loop. The reporter's hunch was right but the persistence wasn't in the merge queue — it was in `.task/reviews/merge.json`.

Root cause of the replay loop:
- During the original round-1 failure, `WorkflowState._write_merge_failure_review('merge_error', 'advance_main failed (stash_failed)…')` (workflow.py:1142-1163) wrote a stale verdict to `.task/reviews/merge.json` in this worktree.
- That file persists across workflow re-runs. When the steward resolves a level-0 escalation, the workflow re-queues the task to `pending` (workflow.py:1542). The next cycle's review phase reads ALL `.task/reviews/*.json`, picks up the stale `merge.json`, and re-emits the same blocking `merge_error` — even though main already contains `d7daa9841d` and the merge queue itself has no persistence.
- That's why every round produced an identical "advance_main failed (stash_failed)" blocker despite the merge having landed: the replay was driven by an on-disk review artifact, not the merge queue's runtime state.

What I did:
- Re-verified `git merge-base --is-ancestor d7daa9841d main` → still true.
- Confirmed merge_queue.py is in-memory only (no `data/queue/` or `data/orchestrator/` persistence directories exist).
- Inspected `.task/reviews/` and found the stale `merge.json` with verdict ISSUES_FOUND / `advance_main failed (stash_failed)`.
- Deleted `.task/reviews/merge.json`. Remaining review files are the legitimate per-reviewer artifacts (architect_reviewer, performance, reuse_auditor, robustness, test_analyst).
- Re-asserted `set_task_status(id=514, status=done)` (returned `no_op: true` — already done from the previous steward turn).

Expected behavior on next workflow cycle (if any):
- Review phase will not pick up the stale merge_error.
- Merge phase will see `is_ancestor(branch_head, main_sha)` → emit `already_merged` → success → workflow marks the task done normally.

Note for design: `_write_merge_failure_review` should probably also write a success/cleared file when a subsequent merge attempt succeeds, or the review reader should ignore `merge.json` older than the current branch tip. Without that, any task that escalates from a transient merge failure will replay the failure forever once steward starts resolving its escalations. (Did not file a follow-up task — out of scope for this escalation, but worth surfacing.)
workflow_state: step-7 verification complete; all 9 checks passed; no file changes; escalation filed
